title: Gitvana
subtitle: THE FINAL EXAM — Recover, clean, cherry-pick, merge, tag

briefing.narrative:
The Hall of Ancestors is silent. Every monk in the monastery has gathered. The cat sits on the highest shelf, wearing a crown made of ethernet cables.

The Head Monk steps forward. His voice echoes through the chamber.

"You have walked the path of init. You have weathered merge conflicts that would break lesser souls. You have traveled through time, recovered the lost, and split the unsplittable."

He gestures to a massive screen showing a repository in ABSOLUTE CRISIS:

- A dev branch with 3 critical commits (network, monitoring, backup) was DELETED. Those commits exist only in the reflog now.
- The feature-enlightenment branch has 7 commits: 3 good (enlightenment module, wisdom DB, zen mode), 2 WIP, 1 leaked secret, and 1 hotfix that belongs on the hotfix branch.
- The hotfix branch exists but has nothing on it yet.
- Main needs ALL the good work: recovered dev commits, cleaned feature, and the hotfix.

Five cascading problems. Each depends on solving the previous one.

"Recover the lost commits. Clean the feature branch. Move the hotfix. Merge everything. Tag v3.0."

The cat purrs. It already believes in you.

"Do all of this... and you will have achieved GITVANA."

He sits in lotus position. "Total mastery of version control. The end of suffering. Or at least, the end of Git suffering."
briefing.concept:
This final challenge combines every skill you've learned:

- Reflog: recover lost commits from deleted branches
- Reset + cherry-pick: clean up messy branches, remove secrets
- Cherry-pick: move commits between branches
- Merge: bring branches together
- Tag: mark the final release

The key is to triage: assess all damage first, plan your approach, then execute one step at a time. Every step depends on the previous one being correct. There is no shortcut.
objectives: Recover the 3 lost dev commits from reflog (network, monitoring, backup)
Clean the feature branch: keep only the 3 good enlightenment commits
Move the hotfix commit from feature to the hotfix branch
Merge recovery, hotfix, and cleaned feature branches into main
Tag the final result as v3.0
No secrets or WIP files in the final result
